issues.objection: disagreements_among_translators in 7.22 carries an explanation that names the substantive divergence between Advaita and Vishishtadvaita readings (and where applicable, the Gaudiya register), without flattening either side. The engineering layer respects the convergence and tags STRETCHED where the verse extends beyond operational scope. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice.

notes: Disagreement-explanation review for 7.22: explanation field is non-trivial and references the specific point of doctrinal divergence. PASS.
